title: Veggie Chili with Skillet Cornbread
description: A hearty and flavorful vegetarian chili, perfect for a family meal, served with warm, golden skillet cornbread. This one-pan chili is easy to make and kid-friendly.
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add chopped onion and red bell pepper; cook until softened, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic, chili powder, cumin, and smoked paprika. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.

Add diced tomatoes (undrained), rinsed kidney beans, rinsed black beans, vegetable broth, 0.5 tsp salt, and 0.25 tsp black pepper. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat and let it cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
While chili simmers, preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Grease a 9-inch cast-iron skillet or baking dish.
In a medium bowl, whisk together cornmeal, flour, baking powder, sugar, and 0.5 tsp salt. In another bowl, whisk together milk, egg, and 0.25 cup vegetable oil.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined. Do not overmix.
Pour the cornbread batter into the prepared skillet. Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown and a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Stir the frozen corn into the chili during the last 5 minutes of cooking. Serve the chili hot with slices of warm skillet cornbread.
tips: For a thicker chili, mash some of the beans against the side of the pot with a spoon.
Customize the chili with your family's favorite vegetables, such as zucchini or sweet potatoes.
Leftover chili and cornbread can be stored in airtight containers in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days.
